proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; Claim: totalSignals >= fileCount at line 109.
; The loop increments fileCount by 1 and totalSignals by signals.length (>= 1)
; in exactly the same branch: if (signals.length > 0).
; Neither counter can exceed the other in the direction fileCount > totalSignals.
(declare-const fileCount Int)
(declare-const totalSignals Int)
(declare-const counted_files Int)   ; iterations where try succeeded AND signals.length > 0
(declare-const signals_sum Int)     ; sum of signals.length across counted_files iterations
; Transitions modelling the loop body:
;   fileCount  = counted_files          (incremented once per qualifying iteration)
;   totalSignals = signals_sum          (incremented by signals.length per qualifying iter)
;   signals_sum >= counted_files        (each qualifying file contributes >= 1 signal)
(assert (= fileCount counted_files))
(assert (= totalSignals signals_sum))
(assert (>= signals_sum counted_files))
(assert (>= counted_files 0))
(assert (>= signals_sum 0))
; Negation of property: totalSignals < fileCount
(assert (< totalSignals fileCount))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat
; Proof: signals_sum >= counted_files = fileCount, so totalSignals = signals_sum >= fileCount.
; The negation is unsatisfiable — the co-mutation pattern guarantees this.

proven.claim: Claim: fileCount cannot exceed the number of files iterated.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; Claim: fileCount cannot exceed the number of files iterated.
; The for-of loop runs exactly tsFiles_length iterations. Each iteration
; either: (a) throws and is caught [exceptions], (b) produces no signals
; [no_signals], or (c) produces signals and increments fileCount [fileCount].
; These three categories partition all iterations.
(declare-const tsFiles_length Int)
(declare-const fileCount Int)
(declare-const exceptions Int)   ; iterations where try block threw
(declare-const no_signals Int)   ; iterations where signals.length == 0
; Transition: partition of all loop iterations
(assert (>= tsFiles_length 0))
(assert (>= fileCount 0))
(assert (>= exceptions 0))
(assert (>= no_signals 0))
(assert (= tsFiles_length (+ fileCount exceptions no_signals)))
; Negation: fileCount > tsFiles_length
(assert (> fileCount tsFiles_length))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat
; Proof: fileCount is one term in the sum = tsFiles_length; it cannot exceed the total.

proven.claim: Claim: if totalSignals is zero at line 109, fileCount must also be zero.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; Claim: if totalSignals is zero at line 109, fileCount must also be zero.
; Both counters are incremented exclusively in the same if-branch; if
; no file contributed signals, neither counter was touched.
(declare-const totalSignals Int)
(declare-const fileCount Int)
(declare-const counted_files Int)
(declare-const signals_sum Int)
; Transitions: loop accumulation
(assert (= fileCount counted_files))
(assert (= totalSignals signals_sum))
(assert (>= signals_sum counted_files))   ; each counted file contributes >= 1
(assert (>= counted_files 0))
(assert (>= signals_sum 0))
; Negation: totalSignals = 0 AND fileCount > 0 simultaneously
(assert (= totalSignals 0))
(assert (> fileCount 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat
; Proof: fileCount > 0 implies counted_files > 0, implies signals_sum >= 1,
; implies totalSignals >= 1, contradicting totalSignals = 0.

violations.claim: Reachable: totalSignals = 0, fileCount = 0 at line 109 while tsFiles_length > 0.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; Reachable: totalSignals = 0, fileCount = 0 at line 109 while tsFiles_length > 0.
; readFileSync, parser.parse, or signalRegistry.findAll can all throw.
; The catch block is empty — every exception is absorbed without diagnostic output.
; The init then prints "Found 0 signals across 0 files" and exits normally,
; masking parse failures and misreporting zero coverage.
(declare-const tsFiles_length Int)
(declare-const exceptions Int)
(declare-const fileCount Int)
(declare-const totalSignals Int)
; Transition: git ls-files succeeded and returned files (tsFiles is non-empty)
(assert (> tsFiles_length 0))
; Transition: every loop iteration throws — all files are caught and skipped
(assert (= exceptions tsFiles_length))
; Result: accumulator variables remain at their initial values of 0
(assert (= fileCount 0))
(assert (= totalSignals 0))
; Violation condition: zero-count output while files were actually found
(assert (> tsFiles_length 0))
(assert (= totalSignals 0))
(assert (= fileCount 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
; Witness: tsFiles_length=5, exceptions=5 → fileCount=0, totalSignals=0
; The empty catch silently consumes all failures; the init reports no signals
; and installs the git hook anyway — certifying an unchecked codebase.
